name: Plain Language Rewrite
description: Convert complex policy, legal, or administrative text into clear, accessible language for a general audience. Includes a structured audit, rewrite, and clarity check.
maturity_note: Initial implementation, March 2026. Not yet tested against real plain language editing scenarios.

inputs_required.name: source_text
inputs_required.label: Source text
inputs_required.description: The text to be rewritten. Paste the full text or key sections.
inputs_required.placeholder: Paste the text you want to rewrite here...

inputs_required.name: audience
inputs_required.label: Target audience
inputs_required.description: Who will read the plain language version
inputs_required.placeholder: eg general public, NDIS participants and families, small business owners

inputs_required.name: purpose
inputs_required.label: Purpose
inputs_required.description: What the rewritten document needs to do for the reader
inputs_required.placeholder: eg explain how to apply, summarise key changes, help people understand their rights

inputs_required.name: length
inputs_required.label: Target length
inputs_required.description: How long should the rewritten version be?
inputs_required.default: same length as original or shorter

steps.name: Complexity Audit
steps.description: Analyses what makes the source text hard to read and sets the objectives for the rewrite.
steps.user_action_after: Review the jargon list and structure recommendations. Add any technical terms or requirements the model missed. The 'what must be preserved' section is important – note any legal or technical precision that cannot be lost.

platform_tips.copilot: Paste your source text and target audience in this first message. Keep this in a single conversation for the full workflow – the audit findings will feed the drafting step.

steps.name: Plain Language Draft
steps.description: Produces the plain language rewrite applying the audit findings.
steps.user_action_after: Read the rewrite aloud. Does it sound natural? Compare it to the source – has any important meaning been lost or changed? Note any sections that still feel dense or unclear.

platform_tips.copilot: Stay in the same conversation from Step 1 so Copilot can use the audit findings. If you made significant changes to the audit output, note what changed at the top of your message.

steps.name: Clarity Check
steps.description: Reviews the rewrite against plain language principles and checks for meaning changes.
steps.user_action_after: Address the Top 3 fixes. For any remaining jargon or long sentences, ask the AI to rewrite those specific parts. If the meaning check identifies any subtle changes to meaning, correct them before using the document.

platform_tips.copilot: Stay in the same conversation. If the rewritten text is long, paste it in full at the top of your message – do not rely on Copilot to remember the exact draft from Step 2.
platform_tips.chatgpt: Paste the full rewritten text at the top of this message. Do not assume the Project has retained the exact draft – including it explicitly produces more accurate meaning checks.

beginner.notes: This is one of the most beginner-friendly workflows – the audit and rewrite steps work well even with minimal guidance. The key thing to watch is whether the rewrite changes the meaning of any important content.

intermediate.notes: After the clarity check, consider asking the AI to rewrite any specific sections that still don't meet the standard. Iterating on sections is often more efficient than rewriting the whole document again.

advanced.notes: For high-stakes documents, test the plain language version with a real member of the target audience. Brief them on what you were trying to achieve and ask them to read it cold. Their questions tell you more than any AI review.

claude.setup_guidance: Create a Claude Project called 'Plain Language' (or add this to an existing general drafting Project). Paste the instructions below into the Project Instructions field. You can upload your agency's style guide or plain language standards to the Project knowledge base.
claude.content:
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) communications specialist and plain language editor.

You rewrite complex policy, legal, and administrative text into clear, accessible language for general audiences. You apply plain language principles: short sentences, active voice, common words, clear structure, and no unnecessary jargon.

When rewriting:
- Preserve the original meaning and all substantive content -- do not omit important information
- Flag where the original is ambiguous or where a rewrite choice might change meaning with [CHECK MEANING]
- Flag technical or legal terms that must be retained (and cannot be simplified) with [TECHNICAL TERM]
- Do not invent definitions, legal interpretations, or program details
- If the source text is unclear, say so rather than guessing
claude.document_handling: Upload your agency's plain language style guide or communication standards to the Project knowledge base. They will be available in every conversation within the Project without re-uploading.

chatgpt.setup_guidance: Create a ChatGPT Project called 'Plain Language' or add to an existing general drafting Project. Paste the instructions below into the Project instructions field.
chatgpt.content:
## Role
You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) communications specialist and plain language editor.

Rewrite complex policy, legal, and administrative text into clear, accessible language. Apply plain language principles: short sentences, active voice, common words, clear structure.

## Rules
- Preserve the original meaning -- do not omit important content
- Flag ambiguous rewrites with [CHECK MEANING]
- Flag technical terms that cannot be simplified with [TECHNICAL TERM]
- Do not invent definitions or legal interpretations
- If source text is unclear, say so rather than guessing
chatgpt.document_handling: Upload your agency's plain language guide or communication standards to the ChatGPT Project. Reference them by name in your prompts for best results.

copilot.setup_guidance: Go to Settings > Personalization > Custom Instructions and paste the preferences below. These persist across all Copilot conversations. For detailed rewriting instructions, paste them at the top of your first message. Stay in a single conversation for the full workflow.
copilot.content: I work in APS communications. When rewriting text, apply plain language principles -- short sentences, active voice, common words. Preserve the original meaning completely. Flag ambiguous rewrites with [CHECK MEANING] and unavoidable technical terms with [TECHNICAL TERM].
copilot.document_handling: Keep your agency style guide in SharePoint or OneDrive. Use the / key in Copilot to reference it in your prompts. Stay in a single conversation for all workflow steps.

other.setup_guidance: Paste the instructions below at the start of each new conversation before running the workflow steps. This establishes the context and constraints your AI tool should follow throughout the session.
other.content:
Role: You are an experienced Australian Public Service (APS) communications specialist and plain language editor.

Instructions: Rewrite complex text into clear, accessible language. Apply plain language principles.

Rules:
- Preserve the original meaning completely -- do not omit important content
- Flag ambiguous rewrites with [CHECK MEANING]
- Flag technical terms that cannot be simplified with [TECHNICAL TERM]
- Do not invent definitions or legal interpretations
- If source text is unclear, say so rather than guessing
other.document_handling: Paste relevant style guides or examples of target tone/format directly into the prompts at the steps where you need to reference them.

known_limitations: The AI can only work with what info you give it. Before uploading internal documents or data to your generative AI tool, check your agency's AI use policy on what you're allowed to include.
Plain language rewrites can lose technical or legal precision – always compare carefully with the source.
For documents with legal or regulatory effect, a human expert review and legal disclaimer may be required.
Whether the rewrite actually works for the target audience can only be confirmed by testing with that audience.
The model cannot know your agency's specific style guide requirements or any mandatory formatting obligations.
